framework: NTAS98 AstroOdu Body Reading — 48 Orisha Profile-Element Assignments
author: Janaee Lorraine
description: The AstroOdu Body Reading framework recognizes that the nine center Orishas are the structural architecture — the bones of the system. But every person is different, and the specific divine force governing each center varies by individual chart, lineage, and profile-element configuration. This document maps one specific Orisha or Spirit from the African diaspora (Yoruba, Kemetic, Hoodoo, Haitian Vodou, Congolese, Hausa Bori, Akan, Vietnamese, and other traditions) to each of the 48 profile-element combinations.

element_meanings.Fire: Active, executive force, bold action, radiating outward
element_meanings.Earth: Form, physical structure, built into the bones, grounded daily practice
element_meanings.Air: Movement, change, communication, intellectual expression, locomotion
element_meanings.Water: Germinating medium, emotional body, gestates before manifesting, ancestral feeling

missing_profile_note: Elder Warrior (6/3) — Janaee will write the four element entries when ready. Until then, daily snippet falls back to trine-only on Orisha-day rotations for 6/3 users.

entries.profile_description: Deep sacred study meeting lived trial and error — the foundational pathmaker
entries.name: BLACKHAWK
entries.full_text: Blackhawk is the spirit of justice, liberation, and sentinel power rooted in New Orleans Hoodoo tradition. A warrior for justice who intercedes in all legal matters — especially gaining release from unjust imprisonment. Primary spirit guide of Mother Leafy Anderson, founder of the Spiritual Churches of New Orleans, who invoked his aid to overcome legal difficulties and establish her ministry. Associated with the Sauk and Fox war leader who fought with the British in the War of 1812 and led the 1832 uprising known as the Black Hawk War. After his death, his skeleton was placed on public display — and yet his spirit rose more powerful than ever. Place his image near your front door looking outward. He serves as your sentinel and watchman. He guards what you have built and watches over the paths you walk. When you need liberation — from legal trouble, from unjust confinement, from systems designed to hold you — call Blackhawk. He knows what it means to fight for freedom against overwhelming force. Colors: red and black. Attribute: warrior staff.

entries.profile_description: Deep sacred study meeting lived trial and error — the foundational pathmaker
entries.name: AYAO
entries.full_text: Ayao is the Orisha of air, the whirlwind, and the road — Oya's fierce younger sister who resides in both the forest and the eye of the tornado. Like her sister, she is powerful, fierce, and smart. Where Oya commands the marketplace and escorts the dead, Ayao governs the invisible road — the spiritual path that exists before the physical one. She is one of the Orishas of the plant kingdom, deeply fond of botanicals and magical knowledge learned directly from Osain himself — and she may be petitioned to share that knowledge with her devotees. She is received to get rid of spiritual wars and to defend oneself effectively from enemies. She is a fierce warrior who works closely with Osain and is classified as a sanitary warrior alongside Elegba and Ogun. Her presence clears the invisible debris from your path before you walk it. Attribute: crossbow with a serpent. Colors: brown and green — the colors of bark and leaves. Animal: snake. Number: 9.

attributes.colors: brown and green — the colors of bark and leaves

entries.profile_description: Deep sacred study meeting lived trial and error — the foundational pathmaker
entries.name: OCHOSI
entries.full_text: Ochosi is the Orisha of the hunt — but his hunting is interpreted far beyond the literal. He is a central warrior, guardian, and law enforcement officer. As the spirit of justice, he hunts down wrongdoers with the precision of an arrow that never misses its mark. His name derives from a root word for secret — some translate it as left-handed sorcerer. He is a great magician who knows everything about the powers of the forest and botanical medicine, making deadly arrow poison AND its antidote. Obatala's scout, spy boy, and chief of surveillance. He has dominion over confinement — prisons, places where people are caught and trapped — and is positioned to liberate from prison and to prevent imprisonment. Patron of the Maroons — escaped enslaved Africans who created free communities in remote wooded areas. Syncretized to Saint Sebastian. Colors: brown, blue, green, teal, lilac. Numbers: 3, 4, 7, 11. Feast day: January 20th. Offerings left at thresholds of police stations or places of incarceration.

entries.profile_description: Deep sacred study meeting lived trial and error — the foundational pathmaker
entries.name: ABATA
entries.full_text: Abata is the Swamp Queen — Orisha of swamps, marshes, and the threshold where fresh and salt water merge in mangrove territory. She is the female compatriot and wife of Erinle, who governs those same liminal water thresholds. Abata has the power to control the accumulation of wealth or the complete lack thereof — she can open the gates of prosperity or close them. Swamps are associated with secrets and hidden treasure, and so it is unsurprising that Abata is less publicly known than her partner. Those who approach her in the right way find that she guards profound material and spiritual treasure. She may be venerated together with Erinle on a shared altar. She manifests in human or snake form. Her ritual colors are yellow, green, gold, pink coral, and light blue in patterns of 7 and 14 — reflecting the depth and mystery of the waters she rules. Animal: snake.

entries.profile_description: Sacred knowledge expressed through community network building — the oracle of the marketplace
entries.name: PAPA AZAKA
entries.full_text: Papa Azaka is the Lwa of agriculture, earth, and the ancient profound bonds between people and the land. The sacred peasant — the divine country cousin and something of a trickster. He is generous, hardworking, good-natured, and protective when properly fed and respected. But if he considers himself disrespected or underfed, he can be mean and vicious. He is forever testing people — rough manners and ragged clothes are a cover to see how you treat those who appear powerless. He hates the rude and stingy and punishes those who exploit and oppress poor workers. A master herbalist who appears in dreams with advice and instructions — he knows the secrets of the soil. Patron of farmers, laborers, and all those who earn their bread by the sweat of their brow. He strongly prefers rituals performed carefully and correctly. Cash offerings placed on his altar are his permanently — never take them back. He may serve as your bank in emergencies but money must be repaid. Colors: blue and red. Day: Thursday. Number: 3. Animal: field mouse. Attribute: machete, straw bag, pipe, hoe.

entries.profile_description: Sacred knowledge expressed through community network building — the oracle of the marketplace
entries.name: AJE
entries.full_text: Aje is the Orisha of wealth, trade, and cash — manifesting as male or female depending on tradition. In Nigeria generally understood as male; some Cuban traditions consider her female. Those favored by Aje never lack and are never poor. The attribute is the large tiger cowrie shell — finding one unexpectedly is a sign of Aje's protection, favor, and blessings. In West Africa, small white cowrie shells once served as currency, making Aje literally the Orisha who governed the medium of exchange itself. Before Europeans ventured into Africa, Aje gave followers cowrie shells as the instrument of trade. Those who worship her today are granted wealth through cowries, sea shells, gold coins, and all forms of currency. She is a river spirit closely associated with Yemoja, Olokun, and Oshun — the feminine waters of abundance. Aje is the Orisha who ensures that those who align with the principles of righteous wealth are never without what they need. Element: water. Attribute: tiger cowrie shell.

entries.profile_description: Sacred knowledge expressed through community network building — the oracle of the marketplace
entries.name: ANUBIS
entries.full_text: Anubis is the Kemetic Lord of the Dead and the most active participant in the entire death process — and thus in the transition process of all life. He presides over the weighing of the heart ritual in the Hall of Two Truths, placing the heart on the scales of Maat and personally feeding the souls of those who fail to pass to the devouring monster Ammit. He supervised the mummification process — inventing embalming and performing it first on Osiris himself. He guards mediums, ensuring only beloved ancestral spirits approach. He knows the date of everyone's death and may be petitioned to reveal it. He locates lost articles — touch his ears and request his help. He is the conductor of souls to the Field of Celestial Offerings. Favorite people: mediums, diviners, and those working in the funeral industry. He protects children. Color: black. Emblem: bloodstained black and white ox hide on a pole. Star: Sirius the dog star. Animal: jackal/Egyptian hound. Spirit allies: Wepwawet, Isis, Horus, Nephthys, Thoth.

entries.profile_description: Sacred knowledge expressed through community network building — the oracle of the marketplace
entries.name: AFREKE TE
entries.full_text: Afreke Te is the youngest child of Agwe and Naete of the Dahomean sea pantheon — a town named Avlekete near a saltwater lagoon east of the port city of Ouidah is named in her honor. She is the guardian deity of those traveling over water, offering gifts of abundance, knowledge, and fertility. She protects the treasures of the sea. In African diaspora traditions, she has emerged as something of a trickster — playing a role similar to Elegba in some lineages. Synchronized to Archangel Michael. Her favorite people are the multi-digited — those born with extra fingers and/or toes, which are considered marks of her favor and bring good luck and wealth. She may manifest as a woman or a mermaid. The lucky hand root — a root resembling a small human hand with extra fingers — is her sacred amulet, carried to attract wealth and gambler's luck. Place a lucky hand root on her altar and ask her to bless and empower it. Colors: blue and white. Element: saltwater.

entries.profile_description: Natural healing gift called out through community — the called-out medicine
entries.name: BA DEN
entries.alternate_name: The Black Lady / The Holy Mother
entries.full_text: Ba Den, literally the Black Lady, is a small dark-faced porcelain image venerated by Vietnamese and Cambodians — both ethnic groups claim her as their own. Several legends exist about how Ba Den became a goddess and all end in tragedy: she may have committed suicide to avoid an arranged marriage, died resisting assault, fallen from a cliff, or been attacked by a tiger on the mountain. Regardless of the cause of her death, after she died people saw her walking on the mountain chanting Buddhist mantras and prayers. Since the nineteenth century her shrine at Ba Den Mountain has been among the most popular pilgrimage sites in the region. Renowned for healing illness, protecting from danger, and bestowing good fortune and success — she has a generous but volatile nature. All vows made to her must be fulfilled without exception. She is sometimes identified with the Hindu goddess Mariamman. Sacred sites: Ba Den Mountain in Tay Ninh province near the Vietnamese-Cambodian border, and the Bat Pagoda in Soc Trang Province where monks bless vehicles with holy water from her altar. Offerings: fruits of labor, silver, gold.

entries.profile_description: Natural healing gift called out through community — the called-out medicine
entries.name: BABALU AYE
entries.full_text: Babalu Aye is one of the most beloved and feared Orisha in the African diaspora tradition — simultaneously adored and feared, a dread spirit who is tender to those who suffer. His name means Father of the Earth. He is the spirit of smallpox AND its vaccine — he embodies the disease and its cure simultaneously. He has dominion over all skin ailments and infectious diseases, including AIDS, measles, chicken pox, and all illness that manifests on the skin. He owns all secrets of death, disease, and cemeteries. It is considered dangerous to call him by his true name — euphemisms are used to avoid attracting his attention unnecessarily. Babalu Aye strikes down the immoral, arrogant, and wicked. He fells the insolent. Always be kind and polite to even the most decrepit beggar — it could be Babalu Aye testing your character. Strolls with an entourage dressed in red in the heat of noonday sun. Syncretized to Saint Lazarus the poor leper whose dogs lick his sores. Patron of the humble, outcasts, and those who truly suffer. Colors: brown, red, purple, black, white. Number: 17. Animals: scorpions and dogs.

entries.profile_description: Natural healing gift called out through community — the called-out medicine
entries.name: BORI
entries.full_text: The Bori are the indigenous pre-Islamic spirits of the Hausa people of Nigeria and Niger — also called Iska (wind) and Aljan (from Arabic Djinn). The Bori spirits live in an invisible city called Jangare, divided into twelve houses by family, ethnicity, and occupation. There are an infinite number of Bori but approximately one hundred to three hundred are actively involved with human life. Each Bori is associated with specific botanicals, colors, numbers, offerings, fragrances, rhythms, songs, and dances — and each engages in ritual possession. Bori spirits live like people: they get married, have children, and belong to families. They are invoked to bless new beginnings — homes, businesses, marriages, births. Each Bori is associated with specific ailments they have the power to cause AND cure. Illness caused by a Bori is essentially that spirit's calling card — a signal of their interest in a person requiring initiation and relationship. Women hold prominent positions in Bori spiritual traditions. Bori healing rituals are simultaneously initiations. Iron repels the Bori — even the spoken word iron may send them away.

entries.profile_description: Natural healing gift called out through community — the called-out medicine
entries.name: CALUNGA
entries.full_text: Calunga is a Congolese spirit of death and the sea — the word Calunga literally means sea but encompasses far more than saltwater. In Congolese cosmology, Calunga also refers to the realm of the ancestors. The realm of death in many African traditions is located beneath the sea, and goddess Calunga presides over this realm. The Calunga Line is the watery frontier between the visible and invisible worlds — the threshold between the realms of life and death. Calunga rules that line and controls that frontier. There are no barriers to Calunga's power. She transcends all divisions that stymie others, human and spirit alike. She was once considered a supreme deity — remaining the epitome of the protective mother who is so powerful she cannot help but be threatening even when she does not seek to be. She crossed the waters from Central Africa to Brazil with the slave trade, emerging in Brazil as both Calunga Grande (the ocean) and Calunga Pequena (the graveyard). She guards the boundary between the world of the living and the world of the dead. Element: water.

entries.profile_title: HEALING CHIEF Natural healing gift projected onto as semi-divine authority — the semi-divine remedy
entries.name: DANKO DAN MUSO
entries.alternate_name: The Sudden Visitor / The Drawer of the Belly
entries.full_text: Danko Dan Muso is the head of the Bori snake spirits. His name is an affectionate honorific meaning honored little son of Moses — the biblical lawgiver and snake master, also identified with the Congolese snake spirit Simbi and Vodou Damballah. He is a freshwater snake who lives in rivers, streams, and wells. Visitors to these places are cautioned not to disturb him or to intrude on his privacy. He is a shape-shifter and may appear in forms other than serpentine. It is crucial to always be very polite near freshwater — especially to strangers. Danko Dan Muso has access to abundant wealth which he distributes to human devotees as he pleases. He possesses mediums easily, rapidly, and abruptly — hence his epithet the Sudden Visitor. He is a healer but also possesses a large repertoire of ailments with which to afflict people. He causes stomach ailments, digestive disorders, female infertility, and blindness by spitting into the victim's eyes — a trick he learned from his wife Kwakiya the Black Headed Cobra. Colors: black and indigo. Tree: African ebony (jackal berry tree). Offerings: raw eggs and raw liver — he is always insatiably hungry.

attributes.offerings: raw eggs and raw liver — he is always insatiably hungry

entries.profile_title: HEALING CHIEF Natural healing gift projected onto as semi-divine authority — the semi-divine remedy
entries.name: CANDELO SEDIFE
entries.full_text: Candelo is an extremely popular Dominican Lwa originating on the island of Hispaniola. Once upon a time he may have been an enslaved African who labored on a sugar plantation — a font of knowledge regarding African religious, spiritual, and mystic traditions. Others came to consult him and benefit from his expertise. He also made and sold potent firewater which drew people to him. Candelo is now a powerful spirit who is no longer enslaved — he roams Dominican streets at night playing dice and cards and attending cockfights. An old wise miracle worker who may theoretically be petitioned for anything but specializes in improved luck, business, and finances. He is invoked to ward off or alleviate poverty. A classicist who strongly prefers rituals performed carefully and correctly. He has a temper — he is not a spirit for the dabbler or the inexperienced. He loves dancing and likes the ladies. Because Candelo essentially IS fire, extreme caution is required with fire safety when honoring him — he announces his presence with conflagrations. Protects women and children. Patron of cockfighters and those born under fire signs. Colors: red. Bird: rooster. Attribute: machete. Offerings: rum or Florida Water set alight, cigars or cigarettes lit and presented.

attributes.offerings: rum or Florida Water set alight, cigars or cigarettes lit and presented

entries.profile_title: HEALING CHIEF Natural healing gift projected onto as semi-divine authority — the semi-divine remedy
entries.name: AL BURAQ
entries.full_text: Al Buraq is a winged white horse — bigger than a donkey but smaller than a mule — with the crowned head of a beautiful woman and the wings and tail of a glorious male peacock. She is the sacred mount on which the Prophet rode on his miraculous flight from Mecca to Jerusalem and then to the Seventh Heaven. Her origins are unclear — some scholars speculate she is related to ancient spirits of Mesopotamia depicted with human heads on large animal bodies, possibly a form of angel. Her image serves as a powerful amulet for personal safety and for flying over obstacles that seem impassable. Historically her images are mass-produced as popular chromolithographs — highly unusual in a culture that discourages visual depiction, testifying to her widespread protective power. In Pakistan, buses are ornamented with her image for safety in travel. In Muslim West Africa, merchants post her image in hopes she will serve as a vehicle for miraculous business and entrepreneurial success. Her power is used similarly to that of Mami Wata and La Sirene in other traditions — she carries people over troubled waters into abundance.

entries.profile_title: HEALING CHIEF Natural healing gift projected onto as semi-divine authority — the semi-divine remedy
entries.name: SIMBI / CYMBEE
entries.origin: Congolese — South Carolina Low Country / African Diaspora
entries.full_text: Simbi is a family of Congolese water spirits who survived the Middle Passage from Africa and arrived in South Carolina's Low Country — where they slipped into the freshwater springs and creeks and made themselves at home. In the nineteenth century, folklorists recorded Cymbee stories from formerly enslaved people who spoke of these spirits with cautious reverence — the word Cymbee is now used to distinguish South Carolina manifestations. These written reports emphasized that local Black people were frightened of the Cymbees — but this fear was sacred respect, not superstition. Cymbee spirits are sensitive to what they perceive as disrespect. Each spring or small body of fresh water has its own Cymbee with its own unique appearance, size, and habits — individual Cymbees have names, some wonderfully evocative like The Great Desire of the Unrotting Waters. Whether in Africa or America, Cymbees are guardians of water. The Middle Passage did not improve their tempers. They show displeasure via roiling and churning waters, raising storms, and by removing water — springs drying up signal the departure or disapproval of the Cymbee. They bring prosperity, good fortune, abundance, and fertility. Proportionate in size to their water source. Offerings: treat their water as precious and sacred. Small shiny trinkets and traditional mermaid offerings appreciated.

attributes.offerings: treat their water as precious and sacred

entries.profile_description: Lived battle experience expressed through authoritative community leadership — the executioner of order
entries.name: EXHU
entries.full_text: Exu is the Brazilian evolution of Eshu Elegbara — the Master of Roads in his most volatile, untamed, and powerful New World expression. In Brazil he evolved into a category unto himself: there are many Exus, many individual aspects of one limitless force. Each Orisha has its own Exu serving as a private messenger between the divine and the human. He determines whether someone's path is clear or blocked with obstacles — he determines how easy or how challenging an individual's life will be. A miracle healer who provides opportunities and good fortune. Fears nothing — there is no road he is afraid to travel. No illness he cannot cure. Invoke Exu to scare away the forces that frighten you and to protect you from your own evil impulses. He reads your heart — if you are afraid of him and expect the worst, he may fulfill your expectations. But for those who approach him with humor and sincerity, he can be a dedicated guardian and very benevolent provider. He signals his presence with the scent of sulphur. Colors: red and black. Numbers: 3 and 7. Plant: wormwood (Absinto). Offerings: tobacco, sugarcane, honey, Cachaça, absinthe, rum steeped with hot peppers.

attributes.offerings: tobacco, sugarcane, honey, Cachaça, absinthe, rum steeped with hot peppers

entries.profile_description: Lived battle experience expressed through authoritative community leadership — the executioner of order
entries.name: EZILI DANTOR
entries.full_text: Ezili Dantor is Ezili Freda's hardworking black sister — independent and beholden to no one. She is sick of crying. She has had enough of tears. She is a spirit of rage and initiative. Honored as the spirit who initiated the Haitian Revolution — she fought alongside the men. Her lover and partner was Ogoun, who at the revolution's end cut out her tongue so she could not reveal secrets. Guardian of women, especially single mothers. Ask her to help you become so financially self-sufficient you need no one else's money. Ask her for help if you are trapped in an abusive relationship. She punishes or expresses anger through shooting and stabbing pains. She may cause rapists to hang themselves. Syncretized to the Black Madonna of Czestochowa — the scratches on her face were inflicted by her sister Ezili Freda. Favored people: single mothers, working women, market women, lesbians, female soldiers, stroke victims. Manifestation: sturdily built dark-skinned woman with possible facial scars holding a child in one hand and a knife in the other. Animal: Haitian black pig. Colors: red and blue. Days: Tuesday and Saturday. Offerings: cigarettes, Barbancourt rum, pineapples, sweet red wine, knives, daggers, fried pork, corn products with gunpowder, honey with cinnamon and cayenne.

attributes.offerings: cigarettes, Barbancourt rum, pineapples, sweet red wine, knives, daggers, fried pork, corn products with gunpowder, honey with cinnamon and cayenne

entries.profile_description: Lived battle experience expressed through authoritative community leadership — the executioner of order
entries.name: ESHU ELEGBARA
entries.full_text: Eshu Elegbara is the master of the crossroads — lord of communications and guardian of gates. He is the owner of all roads, paths, and thresholds, both literal and metaphoric. He owns the highway, the path to your door, and the Road to Success. He opens and closes roads, removes obstacles from your path, and obstructs enemies and misfortune so they cannot reach you. An intense trickster and force for justice — a lover of truth that nothing can be hidden from. Mediates between people and all other spirits. He is the first spirit invoked in every African diaspora ceremony — Legba opens the gate for all other spirits to follow. Nothing can be communicated to the divine without his permission and participation. Blesses devotees with the gift of manual dexterity — beloved by musicians, gamblers, dice players, and practitioners. He is over 121 paths and manifestations. In Africa originally venerated as a handsome virile young man. In the Western Hemisphere he manifests as a merry tricky little boy or an old limping man weary from walking the world. Colors: red and black. Number: 3. Animal: mouse. Planet: Sun. Day: Monday. Offerings: rum, candy, cigarettes, toys, food laced with hot sauce and peppers.

attributes.offerings: rum, candy, cigarettes, toys, food laced with hot sauce and peppers

entries.profile_description: Lived battle experience expressed through authoritative community leadership — the executioner of order
entries.name: INNA
entries.full_text: According to some versions of Hausa myth, Inna is the mother of ALL Bori spirits. She is among the most powerful of the Bori — a protective deity who guards her devotees as if they were her children. She may be invoked to protect property of those in her favor. One of the two wives of Sarkin Aljan Suleimanu — she is also involved in a not-so-secret love affair with Kuturu the Leper, her husband's chief counselor. Her brother is the Head of the Bori House of Fulani Spirits and she is envisioned as a Fulani woman. Inna punishes thieves and those who persecute her devotees by causing stomachs to fatally swell. Her traditional affliction is paralysis — a very specific, rapid-progressing form that presents as one hand and foot twisted, one hip rigid, eyes bulging, mouth paralyzed open on one side. This paralysis caused by Inna will resist conventional medical treatment and must be dealt with shamanically immediately as it can be fatal if left untreated. Manifests as a tall, dark-skinned, beautiful, regal woman. May be represented by the image of the snake charmer. Sacred trees: baobab, African ebony, African mahogany, shea tree. Color: black.

entries.profile_title: WARRIOR ELDER Trial and error path toward sagehood through lived experience — the grounded shield
entries.name: OGUN
entries.origin: Yoruba — West Africa / Santería / Candomblé / Haitian Vodou
entries.full_text: Ogun is the first Orisha to descend from heaven to earth — Osin Imole, the first of the primordial Orishas to come to the physical world. When the Orishas were preparing to descend to make the Earth habitable, a dense impenetrable forest blocked their way. All the other Orishas tried and failed. Ogun stepped forward, produced a powerful iron machete, and effortlessly cut down the trees and cleared the roots — creating the very first path and allowing civilization to begin. He is the deity of iron, war, hunting, metalwork, metallurgy, technology, and divine judgment. The patron of blacksmiths, warriors, hunters, drivers, surgeons, and all who work with metal or tools. He clears paths both literally and metaphorically — his force makes new beginnings possible. He is the Lord of Feet, representing the part of the body that allows humans to move forward. He has a complex, dual nature: creator and destroyer, craftsman and warrior, pathway-maker and boundary-setter. In Haitian Vodou he is Ogou Feray — the god of war and pioneering intelligence, carrying an iron saber and wearing a red sash. Colors: green and red (Nigeria), black (Yorubaland), red sash (Haiti). Day: Tuesday. Tools: machete, iron implements.

attributes.colors: green and red (Nigeria), black (Yorubaland), red sash (Haiti)

entries.profile_title: WARRIOR ELDER Trial and error path toward sagehood through lived experience — the grounded shield
entries.name: NANA BURUKU
entries.origin: Yoruba / Fon / Dahomean — West Africa / African Diaspora
entries.full_text: Nana Buruku is the primordial mother — grandmother of ALL the Orishas, older than creation itself. In Dahomey mythology she is the supreme mother creator who gave birth to Mawu the moon spirit, Lisa the sun spirit, and all of the universe. After giving birth she retired, leaving the matters of the world to Mawu-Lisa — but her authority underlies everything. She is the ancient root Lwa, the eldest of the watery Orishas. Lady of marshes, swamps, mud, and clay — she presides over the primeval swamp from which life first emerged. An old fierce swamp witch who is an austere, severe spirit — in Africa her altars are traditionally maintained outside because she is considered too volatile to safely bring indoors. She is extremely generous with those she loves. A fearless warrior who is petitioned for victory. Divine herbalist who presides over medicinal plants and may be petitioned to discover herbal solutions to health problems. She appears in the guise of an incredibly old woman and travels in the form of a freshwater snake. She carries a special staff constructed from palm fronds decorated with cowrie shells. Lives in swamps and earthen mounds. Sacred number: 7. Feast: July 26.

entries.profile_title: WARRIOR ELDER Trial and error path toward sagehood through lived experience — the grounded shield
entries.name: OSAIN
entries.full_text: Osain is the Orisha who owns ALL plants and herbs. Before gathering or using any plant, herbalists must ask Osain's permission and make offerings. He is the supreme herbalist — possessing unparalleled knowledge of the medicinal and spiritual uses of every plant on earth. Without him, none of the Orisha ceremonies can happen: his magic and the herbs he governs are used to conjure the shrines dedicated to all other Orishas. He is the only deity able to use cornmeal oil to produce medical ointment. Depicted as a disfigured impish man with one eye, one hand, one foot — one tiny ear that hears a pin drop and one enormous ear that hears nothing. This liminality is intentional: he is the figure between worlds, between what heals and what harms, between the known and the secret. He is Shango's godfather — gave young Shango a gourd that allowed him to breathe fire and realize his destiny. His symbol is an iron staff with seven prongs topped by a bird that collects knowledge and communicates it to Osain. Syncretized to Saint Joseph. Colors: green (nature and growth), brown (earth), white (spiritual clarity). Number: 7. Day: Thursday. Offerings: tobacco, coins, alcoholic beverages like Aguardiente left at sites where botanicals were harvested.

attributes.colors: green (nature and growth), brown (earth), white (spiritual clarity)
attributes.offerings: tobacco, coins, alcoholic beverages like Aguardiente left at sites where botanicals were harvested

entries.profile_title: WARRIOR ELDER Trial and error path toward sagehood through lived experience — the grounded shield
entries.name: MAMI WATA
entries.origin: Pan-African — West Africa / Central Africa / African Diaspora
entries.full_text: Mami Wata is not a single deity but a vast family of water spirits known across Western Africa, Central Africa, Eastern Africa, and into the African diaspora — whose name in pidgin English means Mother Water or Mistress Water. Often portrayed as a mermaid, a crocodile, or a snake charmer, she is at once beautiful, jealous, generous, seductive, and potentially deadly. Her power increased between the fifteenth and twentieth centuries during the era of growing international trade — she became the spirit who governed the waters of commerce and the crossing of boundaries. Africans forcibly carried across the Atlantic as part of that trade brought their beliefs and practices honoring Mami Wata and other water spirits with them. She can bring good fortune in the form of money. She is a healer and a taker. To receive her blessings you must surrender something to her — she often demands exclusivity, asking devotees to abstain from other relationships in exchange for her gifts. She manifests as a beautiful woman combing her hair, sometimes with the lower body of a fish or snake. Related to La Sirene, Mae d'Agua, Maman de l'Eau, and Watra-mama across the diaspora. Colors: red and white, or bright gemstone colors. Attribute: mirror and comb.

attributes.colors: red and white, or bright gemstone colors

entries.profile_description: Community networks maturing into ancestral guardianship — the steward of memory
entries.name: SHANGO
entries.origin: Yoruba — West Africa / Santería / Candomblé / Haitian Vodou
entries.full_text: Shango was the fourth Alaafin (king) of the Oyo Empire — a powerful ruler with a voice like thunder and a mouth that spewed fire. His reign was marked by continuous military campaigns and battles. When a subordinate challenged his rule and his subjects deserted him, the defeated king left Oyo and according to tradition ascended to the heavens on a golden chain rather than dying an ordinary death. His followers proclaimed he had transformed into an Orisha. He later took on the attributes of the preexisting thunder deity Jakuta, who represented the wrath of God. The Shango cult became integral to the installation of every Oyo king and spread through the entire Yoruba diaspora. He rules over thunder, lightning, fire, drumming, dancing, justice, virility, and leadership. He strikes down the immoral, arrogant, and wicked with lightning. He punishes liars and wrongdoers. He is also a patron of joy — his energy inspires celebration and the understanding that passion aligned with truth becomes divine power. His thunder in the African diaspora is still heard — in the drum circles of the Caribbean, in Candomblé ceremonies, in the Shango Baptists of Trinidad. Colors: red and white. Symbol: double-headed axe (Oshe). Numbers: 4, 6. Syncretized to Saint Barbara (Cuba) and Saint Jerome (Brazil).

entries.profile_description: Community networks maturing into ancestral guardianship — the steward of memory
entries.name: YEMOJA
entries.origin: Yoruba — West Africa / Santería / Candomblé / Haitian Vodou
entries.full_text: Yemoja is the Great Ocean Mother — patron spirit of rivers and oceans, and the mother of all Orishas and all humanity. Her name is a contraction of Yeye Omo Eja meaning Mother Whose Children are the Fish — reflecting the vastness of her motherhood and her dominion over all living things. She is motherly and strongly protective, caring deeply for all her children, comforting them and cleansing them of sorrow. She does not easily lose her temper — but when angered, she can be as destructive and violent as the flood waters of turbulent rivers. She governs everything pertaining to women: parenting, child safety, love, and healing. When her waters broke according to some traditions, a great flood created rivers and streams and the first mortal humans were created from her womb. She is believed to cure infertility in women — cowrie shells represent her wealth. In traditional Yorubaland she was the deity of the Ogun River. In the diaspora she became the Ocean Mother — patron of those who cross the waters. Her festivals see millions of Brazilians dressed in white throwing white flowers into the sea on New Year's Eve. Colors: blue and white. Number: 7. Day: Saturday. Offerings: white flowers, watermelon, white roses, seafood.

attributes.offerings: white flowers, watermelon, white roses, seafood

entries.profile_description: Community networks maturing into ancestral guardianship — the steward of memory
entries.name: THOTH
entries.full_text: Thoth is the Kemetic god of wisdom, writing, knowledge, the moon, and the measurement of time — the divine scribe who recorded the deeds of all souls and kept the sacred texts of creation. He is the inventor of writing, language, science, philosophy, and magic. He was present at the weighing of the heart ceremony recording the outcome for Osiris and Anubis. He is the keeper of the Akashic record — the divine record of everything that has ever happened. Sometimes called Hermes Trismegistus in the Greek tradition, Triple Great Hermes — the master of all three worlds: the physical, the mental, and the spiritual. He is one of the few deities who could freely travel between the realm of the living and the realm of the dead without restriction. He protected Ra during his nightly journey through the underworld. He was the patron of all scribes and scholars. In the African diaspora and esoteric traditions, Thoth carries the knowledge of sacred geometry, sacred language, and the divine mathematics that underlie all of creation. His knowledge is not just information — it is the technology of reality itself. Form: ibis-headed man or baboon. Sacred: moon disk, staff, writing palette, scrolls.

entries.profile_description: Community networks maturing into ancestral guardianship — the steward of memory
entries.name: OLOKUN
entries.full_text: Olokun is the ruler of the deepest depths of the ocean — androgynous in nature, viewed as male in some traditions, female in others, and beyond gender in still others, reflecting the ocean's fluid depths. The name means owner of the ocean — and Olokun literally owns all the riches that exist beneath the water's surface. He is believed to be the parent of Aje, the Orisha of great wealth, making Olokun the source of material abundance and prosperity at the deepest level. Within Santería, Olokun symbolizes hidden wealth, the unconscious mind, and the secrets of life and death. Some lineages say the ocean depths are where human souls travel after death and before birth — making Olokun the cosmic gateway between worlds. For Afro-descendant devotees in the Americas, Olokun represents a profound ancestral connection — he is said to care for the souls of those lost in the Middle Passage, making him a central figure in diaspora healing. He must be propitiated carefully because his power is immense and his temper can be landscape-altering — in one myth he rose to drown humanity and had to be chained by Obatala with the longest chain Ogun could forge. Colors: blue, white, deep indigo. Symbols: sea shells, fish, coral, anchors.

entries.profile_description: (JUXTAPOSITION — FIXED FATE / AYANMO) The Pillar of the Oja — fixed sacred foundation meeting community network — immovable transition point
entries.name: ODUDUWA
entries.full_text: Oduduwa is the divine founder-king and creator deity — the first Alaafin of Ile-Ife, the holy city of the Yoruba people. His name Odu-ti-o-da-iwa translates literally as the great repository which brings forth existence. When Obatala failed in his creation mission — becoming drunk on palm wine — Oduduwa took the satchel of creation, descended from heaven on a chain onto the primeval waters, and cast a handful of earth that rose into a mound. He placed a five-toed cockerel on this mound to scatter the earth and create land. He planted a palm nut from which sprang a tree with sixteen branches representing the sixteen founding clans of Ile-Ife — the first city and the cradle of Yoruba civilization. He became the ancestor of all Yoruba crowned kings, and all Yoruba people consider themselves Omo Oduduwa — children of Oduduwa. He is revered as the 401st spirit, the only one that speaks — meaning his authority transcends the entire Orisha pantheon. He is the fixed point from which all other dynasties, all other markets, all other communities emerged. Everything traces back to him. Historically his reign is dated to the Late Formative Period of Ife (800-1000 CE). He is the eternal Pillar.

entries.profile_description: (JUXTAPOSITION — FIXED FATE / AYANMO) The Pillar of the Oja — fixed sacred foundation meeting community network — immovable transition point
entries.name: OBATALA
entries.full_text: Obatala is the oldest Orisha — the King of the White Cloth, Orisha Funfun, referring to purity both physically and symbolically as the light of consciousness. He was entrusted by Olodumare with the task of creating both the earth and human beings. He molds every child in the womb from clay. He is the creator of human form — the Divine Sculptor. He is the father of all Orishas and the owner of all human heads and minds. He owns everything that is white, all silver metals, and the ceiba tree. A central myth describes how he became drunk on palm wine during creation and produced humans with disabilities and physical differences — in his remorse he swore never to drink alcohol again and became the eternal patron and protector of the disabled, albinos, and those with congenital differences. He represents moral clarity, patience, justice, and compassion. He is the Orisha who settles disputes and brings harmony among the other Orishas. Both male and female in his paths — the only Orisha with an equal number of male and female manifestations. Colors: all white. Day: Sunday. Offerings: white rice (unsalted), cocoa butter, cascarilla, cotton, snails, white foods. Strict taboo: absolutely no alcohol, no palm oil, no salt, no spice.

attributes.offerings: white rice (unsalted), cocoa butter, cascarilla, cotton, snails, white foods

entries.profile_description: (JUXTAPOSITION — FIXED FATE / AYANMO) The Pillar of the Oja — fixed sacred foundation meeting community network — immovable transition point
entries.name: OYA
entries.origin: Yoruba — West Africa / Santería / Candomblé / Haitian Vodou
entries.full_text: Oya is the Orisha of the marketplace, storms, wind, lightning, change, transformation, and the dead. She is the only Orisha who fully accompanies Shango in battle and his equal in many respects — she wore pants and a beard to fight like a man in war. She is the guardian of the gate between the living and the dead and escorts souls on their journey from one realm to the other. She rules the marketplace — the Oja Oba — because the marketplace represents this world, and she guides the dead from it to Orun (heaven). Her name means she tore, referring to her association with powerful winds that tear through and transform everything they touch. She is the Orisha of the nine dead — her children are the egungun (ancestral masquerade spirits) that walk among the living. She has eleven manifestations. She is the patron of cemeteries, transformation, and sudden radical change. She is powerful, fierce, and complex — she does not allow stagnation. When she passes through your life, everything that no longer serves you is swept away. Colors: maroon, dark purple, multicolored. Number: 9. Day: Wednesday. Animal: buffalo. Offerings: nine eggplants, nine colored ribbons, rum, kola nuts.

attributes.offerings: nine eggplants, nine colored ribbons, rum, kola nuts

entries.profile_description: (JUXTAPOSITION — FIXED FATE / AYANMO) The Pillar of the Oja — fixed sacred foundation meeting community network — immovable transition point
entries.name: LA SIRENE
entries.full_text: La Sirene is the Haitian Vodou Lwa of the sea, love, beauty, wealth, and the deep mysteries of the water. She is a mermaid spirit — sometimes described as an Ezili of the Waters because like Ezili she brings good luck and wealth from the sea. She rules over the ocean alongside her consort Agwe and is deeply connected to the ancestors who crossed the Middle Passage. Those who become her devotees find themselves called to the water — she claims people, pulling them toward the sea, toward depth, toward what lies beneath the surface. She brings wealth, healing, and the gifts of the deep. She is beautiful, sensual, and powerful — but her love has a price. Those who accept her gifts must be faithful to her and to the water. She communicates through dreams and visions, appearing to her people in watery dreamscapes. She is related to Mami Wata and is part of the vast family of water spirits that traversed the Atlantic with the diaspora. Her colors are blue and white. Symbols: mirror, comb, silver jewelry, fish, conch shell. Offerings: white foods, seafood, perfume, mirrors, fans.

attributes.offerings: white foods, seafood, perfume, mirrors, fans

entries.profile_description: Practical community leadership fueled by sacred ancestral knowledge — the oracle of the people
entries.name: SEKHMET
entries.full_text: Sekhmet is the fierce lioness-headed goddess of Upper Egypt — the Eye of Ra, the destroyer of enemies, the purifier of disease, and the most powerful healing force in the Kemetic pantheon. Her name means Powerful One or She Who Is Powerful. She is simultaneously the goddess of war and destruction AND the goddess of healing and medicine — the same force that causes disease has the power to cure it. In the creation myths, Ra sent her to destroy humanity when they rebelled against divine order, and she nearly annihilated the human race before being stopped. Her priests were the physicians and healers of ancient Egypt, carrying the knowledge that the same force that destroys can heal when properly directed. She breathes fire and heat. She protects sacred boundaries and enforces divine order. She is the patron of physicians, surgeons, and all healers who work with the body's own inflammatory response. She demands courage and precision from those who invoke her — she does not work with the timid or the careless. Attribute: solar disk with uraeus serpent. Colors: red (the color of blood and fire). Sacred animal: lioness. Connected to: Bast, Hathor, Ra, Ptah.

attributes.colors: red (the color of blood and fire)

entries.profile_description: Practical community leadership fueled by sacred ancestral knowledge — the oracle of the people
entries.name: OSIRIS
entries.full_text: Osiris is the Kemetic lord of the dead, resurrection, vegetation, and the cycle of life — the first to die and be resurrected, establishing that death is not an end but a transformation. He was the first king of Egypt, a wise and just ruler who taught humanity agriculture, law, and the sacred arts of civilization. His brother Set murdered him out of jealousy — dismembering his body and scattering the pieces across Egypt. His wife Isis searched every corner of the land, gathered every piece, and through her extraordinary magic, resurrected him long enough to conceive their son Horus. Osiris became the ruler of the underworld and the judge of souls in the Hall of Two Truths. Every human soul must answer to Osiris after death. He represents the promise that if you live righteously — if your heart is light enough to pass Maat's feather — you will be resurrected into eternal life. He is the original dying and rising god. He is the keeper of the sacred records of how each soul lived. Colors: green (representing vegetation and rebirth) and black (the fertile soil of the Nile). Attribute: crook and flail of kingship. Animal: bull. Sacred: djed pillar (stability and the backbone of the world).

attributes.colors: green (representing vegetation and rebirth) and black (the fertile soil of the Nile)

entries.profile_description: Practical community leadership fueled by sacred ancestral knowledge — the oracle of the people
entries.name: ORUNMILA
entries.full_text: Orunmila is the Orisha of wisdom, divination, prophecy, and knowledge — the witness at creation who saw every human soul's destiny before they descended into physical form. His name means Only Heaven Knows Salvation. He alone was present when Olodumare assigned each soul their fate — making him the only Orisha who can reveal to a person what their Ori's original contract contains. He is the keeper of the 256 Odu Ifa — the entire corpus of divine instruction and prophecy. He does not speak directly to his devotees but communicates only through divination: through the Opele chain, through the sacred palm nuts (Ikin), and through the trained voice of the Babalawo. He is the constant companion of Osain. The divinations and ritual recommendations of Ifa almost always involve herbal baths and offerings — without Osain's plants, and without Orunmila's knowledge of which plants to use when, no ceremony is complete. He governs the third eye center in the AstroOdu framework. His day: Wednesday. Colors: green and yellow. Number: 16. Sacred: Ifa tray, palm nuts, Opele chain. He is the most human-loving of all the Orishas — he intercedes for humanity in the council of the Orishas when others would abandon them.

entries.profile_description: Practical community leadership fueled by sacred ancestral knowledge — the oracle of the people
entries.name: ISIS
entries.full_text: Isis is the Great Mother of Magic — the most powerful magician in the entire Kemetic pantheon, described in sacred texts as Weret-Hekau, the Great Enchantress. She found every scattered piece of Osiris after Set's betrayal and murder and resurrected him through the power of her extraordinary magic. She then conceived Horus and raised him in the marshes of the Nile Delta, protecting him from Set's constant attempts to destroy the heir. Her magic was so powerful that she extracted Ra's secret name from him — and with it, his ultimate power. She is the patron of women, motherhood, healing, magic, and all who grieve and must rebuild. In the African diaspora and throughout the ancient Mediterranean world her cult spread further than almost any other deity — she was the universal goddess, the All Mother, the one who could hear every prayer in every language. She is the model of the devoted partner and the fierce protective mother. Her wings shelter the dead. Her tears for Osiris caused the Nile to flood and bring fertility to the land. In Afro-Brazilian traditions she is Oxum (Oshun) in some lineages. Attribute: throne hieroglyph (her name means throne). Colors: blue, gold, and white. Sacred: ankh, sistrum, stars. Animal: kite (the bird of her mourning cry).

attributes.animal: kite (the bird of her mourning cry)

entries.profile_title: CHIEF HEALER Semi-divine authority backed by natural healing talent — the magnetized leader
entries.name: ERINLE
entries.full_text: Erinle is the Orisha of medicine, healing, the forest, rivers, and the threshold where fresh and salt water merge. He is the divine physician of the Orisha pantheon — a healer with deep knowledge of both botanical medicine and sacred ritual healing. He is inseparable from his companion Abata, the Swamp Queen who governs the same liminal water threshold he governs. In Santería, Erinle is consecrated in Ifa in the Oddun Iroso Umbo — Elegua was his Oluwo and Orunmila his Oyugbona, making him both a warrior and a diviner at his foundation. He is given by the Babalawos and carries an Ifa hand used for his ita imale — his divination reading at initiation. He speaks through Yemoja's diloggun (cowrie divination), meaning you must have Yemoja's ear to hear Erinle speak. His foundation is in a sealed clay jar with a sacred load inside, accompanied by a carved doll holding a fishing rod and hook — he is the fisherman who brings what is hidden beneath the surface into the light of healing. He is a dedicated and precise physician. Day: Monday. Colors: blue and yellow. Offerings: fish, fruits, water plants, hunting implements, fishing tools. Sacred site: rivers and the meeting of fresh and salt water.

attributes.offerings: fish, fruits, water plants, hunting implements, fishing tools

entries.profile_title: CHIEF HEALER Semi-divine authority backed by natural healing talent — the magnetized leader
entries.name: DAMBALLA WEDO
entries.full_text: Damballa is the most ancient Lwa — the great sky serpent and father of creation in the Haitian Vodou tradition. He is the embodiment of pure spiritual energy and cosmic balance. He rarely speaks in possession but moves with great serpentine grace and calm. He is the Lwa of peace, clarity, divine purity, and ancestral wisdom — the oldest Rada Lwa who was present before all others. He is always venerated together with his wife Ayida Wedo, the Rainbow — together they form the complete circle of the universe, the serpent and the rainbow intertwined. Damballa does not eat cooked food — he eats only eggs and sweet things like honey. He is also connected to the biblical Moses and the snake spirits of the Bori tradition through the lineage of sacred snake masters across Africa and the diaspora. He is invoked for spiritual cleansing, the healing of the mind and spirit, financial blessing, and the blessing of newborn children. When Damballa possesses someone they move like a serpent — silent, fluid, ancient. His colors are white and silver. Sacred number: 14. Offerings: eggs, white foods, milk, sweet wine, honey, white candles. Day: Sunday. Sacred space: the ceiling of the temple where his snake images are placed.

attributes.offerings: eggs, white foods, milk, sweet wine, honey, white candles

entries.profile_title: CHIEF HEALER Semi-divine authority backed by natural healing talent — the magnetized leader
entries.name: GRAN BWA
entries.alternate_name: Grand Bois / Master of the Great Forest
entries.full_text: Gran Bwa is the Haitian Vodou Lwa of the deep forest, the master of all plant medicine, and the guardian of the sacred space between the human world and the spirit world. His name means Great Wood or Great Tree — he embodies the ancient forest itself. He is the keeper of the deepest botanical knowledge in the Haitian tradition, governing the plants and herbs that hold the most powerful medicine. He is often described as being made entirely of wood — his possession is distinct and wild, marked by a rough bark-like character. He is not easily accessible. He lives deep in the forest away from human settlement. To reach him requires a willingness to go deep — into the wilderness, into oneself, past all comfort and familiar territory. He knows every plant's spiritual properties. He knows which herbs heal, which protect, and which are used for magical workings. He holds the tradition of the maroon communities — the free Africans who escaped into the mountains and forests and rebuilt their spiritual practices in the wilderness. Gran Bwa is their patron. Colors: dark green and brown. Sacred space: deep forest, old trees, crossroads within forested areas. Offerings: rum, tobacco, tree bark, forest herbs.

attributes.offerings: rum, tobacco, tree bark, forest herbs

entries.profile_title: CHIEF HEALER Semi-divine authority backed by natural healing talent — the magnetized leader
entries.name: EZILI FREDA
entries.full_text: Ezili Freda is the Haitian Vodou Lwa of love, beauty, luxury, dreams, and the deep longing that can never quite be satisfied. She is the feminine principle in its most exquisite, complex, and ultimately heartbreaking expression. She is the wealthiest of the Lwa — she loves fine things, expensive perfume, beautiful jewelry, and gold. But no matter how much she is given, she eventually weeps — because what she wants cannot be fully given in this world. She embodies the grief of beauty in a world that cannot contain it. She is the mother of Anaïs. Her love is real and her blessings are extraordinary — but she demands absolute devotion and can be intensely jealous. She enters possession as a refined and sensual woman who moves with grace and expects to be treated like a queen. She then weeps. This weeping is not weakness — it is the sacred grief of love confronting its own limits. She is the patroness of artists, lovers, dreamers, and those who carry a beauty inside them that the world does not fully see. Syncretized to Our Lady of Sorrows and the Mater Dolorosa. Colors: pink, white, gold, and blue. Day: Tuesday and Thursday. Offerings: perfume, gold jewelry, pink and white flowers, champagne, sweet desserts, cosmetics, mirrors, combs.

attributes.offerings: perfume, gold jewelry, pink and white flowers, champagne, sweet desserts, cosmetics, mirrors, combs

entries.profile_description: Three-phase path to ancestorhood expressed through natural healing gift — the living ancestor's medicine
entries.name: EGUNGUN
entries.full_text: Egungun is the sacred ancestral masquerade tradition of the Yoruba people — the living presence of the collective ancestors who return to walk among their descendants at specific sacred times. The Egungun is not one ancestor but the combined force of all the ancestors of a lineage manifesting through a specifically consecrated masquerade costume so complete that no part of the living priest's body is visible. The Egungun cannot be seen as human — they are the ancestors in physical form. They carry the voices, the wisdom, the medicine, and the corrections of all who have come before. Egungun is associated with Oya who gives the ancestors the power to return — she is both the guardian of the gate and the one who calls them back. The Egungun tradition maintains that the ancestors are not gone — they are present, watching, teaching, and protecting. They carry remedies for the living that only the dead can see. When Egungun speaks in ceremony, their voice is the compressed wisdom of generations. Their presence heals what the living cannot heal on their own. The fire expression of the Elder Healer is the ancestor who radiates — whose presence alone transforms the energy of the living. Day: Saturday (Egungun's sacred day). Colors: multicolored fabrics representing all lineages.

entries.profile_description: Three-phase path to ancestorhood expressed through natural healing gift — the living ancestor's medicine
entries.name: ASASE YAA
entries.alternate_name: Asaase Yaa / Aberewa Yaa / Mother Earth
entries.full_text: Asase Yaa is the Earth goddess of the Akan people of Ghana — the oldest deity in the Akan pantheon, predating even Nyame the sky god. Her name means Old Woman Yaa of the Earth or Thursday-born Earth. She is the mother of Anansi the spider-trickster. She is the deity of fertility, truth, the dead, and the womb of the earth that both sustains the living and receives the dead. There are no temples built for her and no priest class dedicated specifically to her service — because she is everywhere at once, indivisible from the earth itself. On Thursdays (her sacred day), it is forbidden to plow or farm the earth in her honor, recognizing her absolute authority over the land that feeds and holds all life. She is the ground beneath every altar, the soil in which every ancestor is laid, and the womb from which every new life emerges. She does not need grand ceremony — she only needs respect. Those who dishonor the earth dishonor her and she withdraws her fertility. Those who honor the earth find that she multiplies everything they plant. Colors: brown and green — the colors of living earth. Day: Thursday. Sacred: all soil, all planting, all acts of burial and cultivation.

attributes.colors: brown and green — the colors of living earth

entries.profile_description: Three-phase path to ancestorhood expressed through natural healing gift — the living ancestor's medicine
entries.name: MARASA
entries.origin: Haitian Vodou — Rada Lwa / Pan-African Diaspora
entries.full_text: The Marasa are the divine twins of Haitian Vodou — considered the first children of God, the most ancient of the Lwa, and the sacred dual principle of existence itself. Every nanchon (spiritual nation) has its own Marasa, reflecting the belief that twins have special powers across the entire African diaspora. The Marasa are not simply two — they embody the principle of duality itself: day and night, known and unknown, here and beyond. To understand the Marasa is to understand that nothing in this world is singular. Every truth has its opposite. Every gift has its shadow. Every healing has its counterpart in the wound that necessitated it. They are sometimes childlike in possession — playful, demanding, easily hurt by perceived favoritism. They must always receive double offerings and double portions or they become jealous and cause disruption. They are among the first Lwa greeted in ceremony, second only to Legba. In the broader African diaspora, the twin principle is sacred across Yoruba (Ibeji), Fon, Congolese, and many other traditions. They represent the sacred split at the heart of creation — the understanding that the world requires two to exist. Colors: red, blue, and dark pink together. Offerings: always in double portions — twice the food, twice the candles, twice the attention.

attributes.colors: red, blue, and dark pink together
attributes.offerings: always in double portions — twice the food, twice the candles, twice the attention

entries.profile_description: Three-phase path to ancestorhood expressed through natural healing gift — the living ancestor's medicine
entries.name: YEWA
entries.alternate_name: Yegua / Orishaoko / La Llorona of the Orishas
entries.full_text: Yewa is the Orisha of the dead, the tomb, and absolute purity — one of the most sacred and rarely seen Orishas in the entire Yoruba pantheon. She governs the inside of the grave, the final resting place of the physical body, and the mystery of what happens to the soul in the moments immediately following death. She is associated with the most profound mysteries of death that cannot be spoken aloud. She is known as a virgin deity — pure, untouched, and absolutely reserved. She does not interact casually with the living. She is the keeper of the ultimate secret that every human being will eventually confront alone. She is deeply connected to Babalu Aye — where he governs disease and the moment of death, she governs what comes after the body is placed in the earth. She is seen as Oya's elder counterpart in the realm of death: Oya escorts the soul from this world, and Yewa receives what remains in the earth. Her devotees are bound by strict codes of purity and silence. She is spoken of rarely and with great reverence. Colors: pink and white. Number: no specific number — her mysteries are beyond counting. Offerings: white flowers, white foods, milk, cool water. Her altar is always kept covered.

attributes.numbers: no specific number — her mysteries are beyond counting
attributes.offerings: white flowers, white foods, milk, cool water
